channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The AP and the Trump administration are due back in court in their fight over White House access
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Associated Press is returning to a federal courtroom on Thursday to ask a judge to restore its full access to presidential events, after the White House retaliated against the news outlet last month for not following President Trump’s executive order to rename the Gulf of Mexic
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge rules prison heat conditions are unconstitutional, but doesn’t require air conditioning
item.description: About two-thirds of Texas prisons are not fully air conditioned, and dozens of inmates have died in the sweltering heat.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Pooja Salhotra, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump nominates Georgia state Sen Brandon Beach for US treasurer
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday nominated Georgia state Sen. Brandon Beach for U.S. treasurer. He has served as a state senator in Georgia since 2013.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court won't lift order that barred Trump administration from deportations under wartime law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court refused Wednesday to lift an order barring the Trump administration from deporting Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador under an 18th century wartime law. A split three-judge panel of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US robotics companies push for national strategy, including a central office, to compete with China
item.description: American robotics companies are pushing for a national robotics strategy, including a central government robotics office, to promote the industry at a time that Beijing is making intelligent robots a top national priority.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang And Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal judge denies Trump admin's effort to ban transgender people from military
item.description: A federal judge denied the Department of Defense's effort to block transgender people from enlisting in the U.S. military under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What we know about Minnesota’s government employee return to office plan
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz has directed workers to report to offices or job sites at least 50 percent of the time beginning in June. Employee unions are pushing back.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Clay Masters, and Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court allows recently incarcerated to serve on juries
item.description: Minnesotans who are on probation or supervised release for felony convictions will soon be allowed to serve on juries, following a recent decision by the Minnesota Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Judge says Justice Department attacked her character to 'impugn the integrity' of US judicial system
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge accused the Justice Department on Wednesday of attacking her character in an effort to undermine the integrity of the judicial system, forcefully pushing back against the Trump administration's criticism of the courts for rulings that blocked parts of the president'
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A bill proposing a near-total abortion ban causes an uproar at Georgia's Capitol
item.description: Protesters on each side of the abortion debate have packed a hallway of Georgia's Capitol during a hearing on a bill that would ban most abortions.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Kramon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration says it will pull back billions in COVID funding from local health departments
item.description: Federal health officials said Tuesday they are pulling back $11.4 billion in COVID-19-related funds for local public health departments and other health organizations throughout the nation. Minnesota stands to lose $226 million.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press and Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: More Americans say NPR, PBS should be federally funded than not: Survey
item.description: The same survey found 32 percent of Democrats say they regularly get news from NPR, vs. 9 percent of Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: House Republican: White House ‘in denial’ about Signal war plans chat
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon (R-Neb.) said the White House is “in denial” over The Atlantic’s explosive report about a text chain with senior-level administration officials — and, inadvertently, a reporter — that discussed plans for a U.S. attack on the Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE says it needs to know the government’s most sensitive data, but can’t say why
item.description: DOGE staffers have skirted privacy laws, training and security protocols to gain virtually unfettered access to financial and personal information stored in siloed government databases.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler and Jenna McLaughlin, MPR News

item.title: NYC Council to vote on new rules for scaffolding, sidewalk sheds
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council is set to take up a series of bills aimed at cracking down on scaffolding and sidewalk sheds.
item.dc:creator: Justin Finch, PIX 11

item.title: DHS suspends green card processing for refugees, asylees
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) said it is pausing review of green card applications for some refugees and asylees, leaving in limbo those who came to the U.S. after fleeing unrest. The directive from U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to halt judge's order to rehire probationary federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Monday to halt a ruling ordering the rehiring of thousands of federal workers let go in mass firings aimed at dramatically downsizing the federal government.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Abortion once more plays a key role in a state political fight, this time in Wisconsin's court race
item.description: The winner of the April 1 election could hold the power to determine the fate of future litigation in Wisconsin over abortion because the outcome of the race will decide whether liberals or conservatives hold a majority on the state’s highest court.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gov. DeSantis, Border Czar Tom Homan discuss immigration enforcement in Sarasota
item.description: It’s no secret that immigration is a key political topic. That’s why it was featured as the subject matter on Thursday in New College of Florida’s The Socratic Stage.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida bill could raise highway speed limits
item.description: Highway speeds in Florida could be raised if a new bill passes through the Florida Legislature and makes it to the governor's desk.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC
